reviews.review_msg: He did a great job explaining everything and his final was very reasonable

Great lectures with the slides posted ahead of time, readings can be a little dense (IMO you can just not read "The Ethical Life"), and he gives you the essay questions on the exam beforehand. In general he was just a pretty good prof.

Do not take this course if you don't have to. He does try to teach and is very passionate about his job, but it wasn't beneficial for the majority of the students. The first quiz went well, but it was all downhill from there. There were a tremendous amount of students that just walked out of the final.

Super unclear lectures. I seriously took nothing away from lectures and relied solely on the textbook because he would just ramble and use examples that made no sense. On top of that, attendance was mandatory. Good luck deciphering what Bruno is saying.

Guidon was not a good prof. He was an unorganized lecturer, most of his instructions were unclear and his lecture slides were riddled with mistakes. Guindon had high expectations for his students but did not set us up for success. His TA's were as confused as us if not more because they were not given clear instructions.

Super strict about attendance. Used tophat instead of canvas for the textbook, slides and assignments which was SUPER CONFUSING and I did not appreciate spending the extra money. Often got sidetracked by questions asked and would stare blankly and pause until he came up with an example. Genuinely cares that you understand and invites questions

Super respectful teacher who does understand where you are coming from. Lectures are mandatory as he doesn't share recordings unless a reason. 

Overall very nice and easier exams
Very good prof , good at what he does. He is just wants us to understand the topic and he does that very well. Attendance was mandatory and was graded as mark. would definitely take him again.

Bruno is outgoing and approachable, though many students found the grading to be tough. Grading scale was determined at the end of the semester, and as long as you do the practice problems/read the chapter (or show up to lecture) you'll do great on the exams. He also does a bunch of practice in class which helps in exams

Very passionate and genuine with his lessons and makes an effort to be lax and relatable to students. Used TopHat which makes you buy the textbook which isn't a huge deal but I was a little peeved. Explains things in a simple manner and frequently encourages questions. He wants you and your peers to succeed. If you interested in the topic, you good
If you take this class with this prof, I'd recommend that you pick a religion and start praying. TA's were also useless.

Solid prof. Lectures do a great job of breaking down the difficult readings. Midterm was fair as long as you studied the slides. Encourages questions from students. Marking seemed harsher than other profs, but that's only because he emphasizes precision in language. Overall, a really solid prof, but you'll have to work for your grade.

Bruno is one of the most helpful profs I have ever met. He is super passionate and knowledgable about the material. Yes, the topics can be a little complicated but he breaks them down super well and makes it really accessible. 10/10 would take again!

Amazing prof! Explains really clearly and gives super super helpful feedback on assignments. Grading and workload were reasonable, and bruno is really understanding, flexible, and caring. made the content really interesting!
Bruno Guindon is a logician who takes pride in his work. It is sad for me to say that his textbook and tests are not something he should be proud of. (Incredibly flawed)

loved bruno he was an amazing prof but the material itself was extremely hard to understand and needed lots of time outside of class to get an understanding of it. took this class hoping for an easy elective but turned out to be the hardest course of my semester, don't take unless ur prepared to spend a lot of time on the course

The prof isn't bad but the material is very hard. If you aren't good with memorizing different rules and all the diff ways you have to use them, don't take this class as a easy elective, also you need to spend 6-8 hours on this class on your own time and do the practice questions from the textbook. Overall, good prof but difficult class

Professor Guindon is a great guy and genuinely wants to see you succeed; however, the course material itself is difficult and requires a lot of time spent out of class to understand the content. tutorials are largely unhelpful, and the textbook did not help me understand the material. DO NOT take this class if you're looking for an easy elective.

Bruno is a good professor. He's knowledgeable and explains concepts well. However, it seemed his personal writing style would get in the way of being objective when grading student's papers. If it isn't the way he thinks a paper should be structured, he's rather inflexible. This is unlike other Philosophy profs. Wouldn't take again for this reason.
Bruno was a great prof who was passionate about logic. This made it more fun to learn the content even though I found it to be quite difficult. Tutorials definitely made all the difference.

Professor Guindon is very knowledgeable. This course was definitely tougher than other 200 level phil classes. There were weekly in-class group questions that had to be done within the hour, so that was pretty intense. Outside of that though, the papers are generally alright, and there are many paper topics provided.

Dr guidon is a really really great professor. He's very understanding and explains concepts really clearly. His lectures are very engaging. He has high standards for your work but as long as you put some effort in it's definitely very possible to do well. He's really helpful in office hours, so definitely recommend discussing assignments with him

Lkes talking a lot in lectures and encourages participation. for the class in general its pretty chill, i didn't do any readings and i still got a good grade. Bruno emphasizes that because this is a 100 level course, you aren't expected to know any philosophy writing styles so if you follow the same format for every paper you will do good.

Bruno was an amazing professor. He worked hard at making sure everyone understood what was going on and answering questions. He gave very clear explanations and instructions for any of the assignments. The mc final was ROUGH though.

Bruno was an amazing prof that was clearly passionate about the class. Reading could be pretty tough but he explained them fairly well. The papers were marked tough but my TA gave great feedback on them. Although I enjoyed the class it is definitely not the GPA booster I thought it would be.

Good prof, talks about some interesting stuff in class. A few assignments with a good amount of reading. Papers can be somewhat difficult if you don't completely get the readings and the final was pretty straightforward but relies on you doing the readings. Overall, Bruno's a kind and caring prof.

Dr. Guindon is an exemplary professor who clearly loves what he does. Although, this course can be heavy at times, and requires thorough reading of the texts to grasp the material---I wouldn't recommend it for non-Philosophy students. Still, Dr. Guindon is accessible during office hours, and is more than willing to accommodate students if need be.

Solid choice for a professor but the lectures were quite boring.The readings and essay prompts were not easy to understand. Final exam was difficult, so don't take this course as a GPA booster. Class average for papers in my tutorial section was a C+.
He was good but TA was not great. He cut marks everywhere, thought it was a GPA booster but turned out to be otherwise. Recommended if no other option.

professor Guindon is amazing, he's a great lecturer and the readings he assigns are actually interesting. He seems passionate when he teaches and I genuinely enjoyed doing the assignments. Watch out for the TAs tho some of them are tough graders and there is generally no consistency between how each of them grades assignments. Would take again

Absolute legend. love this guy, great prof. explains things super clearly and clearly knows his stuff. gives super thorough answers to questions to make sure people understand and makes sure he isn't going too fast for everyone. fair grader too. overall top notch prof.
Professor Guindon was a great lecturer. Essays were difficult to get good grades on. He was always open to questions after class. The final exam was fair.

I love Bruno's lectures. Make sure you complete the readings because they are hard to understand. My TA was a gem. Remember that you're not arguing about your view, you're discussing if the paper makes philosophical sense. A challenging class for me who never took a Phil class, but constantly reviewing the papers & slides is rewarding at the end.

Bruno is a great prof however the TA's will make or break this course for you. There are discrepancies between what each TA thinks is the right responses to the prompts for the papers which can make it hard. Keep up with readings and memorize major points for the final. There is no help from bruno when you work on the papers or specific prompts.

Dr. Guindon was a fantastic professor. The content can be quite difficult at times but between his clear lectures, help in office hours, and textbook (which he co-wrote), the material became very digestible. TA was a huge help as well in tutorial/office hours. Overall difficult but rewarding course, taught by a great prof who wants you to succeed.

Great prof! No textbook required, but the readings provided by the prof are sometimes hard to understand. Lectures and topics and interesting. 3 papers + 1 final exam. Papers are a bit tough. Exam is not too bad as long as you study all the readings. Make sure to keep up w/ readings every week so you dont fall and read 26 readings before the final.

Dr. Guindon is awesome. Logic is scary at first but he explains things really clearly. Lectures can be a bit speedy which can sometimes result in confusion but that's to be expected with the content. He's super helpful in office hours and wants you to do well, plus he's also just a very nice guy. Overall recommend him and the course!

Dr. Guindon is a wonderful professor. I was terrified coming into this course because of what I'd heard about the difficulties of logic but he really wants you to do well. The textbook is clear and helpful and during lectures he explains things super clearly. He makes learning logic fun even for a non-quantitive person like me! Definitely recommend

Do not take this class unless you want to drop your GPA. The TA's mark very hard on the three papers that total 60% of the final grade. The topics are quite boring and very hard to read as some modules had up to 25 pages of small dense writing. The final exam was cumulative and expected that you knew every detail of the readings.

Im not a phil major so I went into this class not knowing as much as everyone else but I still found myself understanding the material well. Read the textbook!! Its actually an enjoyable read imo. Bruno can be kind of wordy in lecture, so the textbook clears things up. Papers were short with many topic choices.
Guindon is a cool guy but he seems to be a bit of a strict marker. Pray you get a good TA because they will help solidify the concepts which Guindon can be a bit verbose about in lectures

Overall, Bruno is a very good, understanding and accommodating prof. He summarizes the readings during the lecturers, so you dont really need to read them (I never did except for when I wrote my papers on that particular topic). He posts his slides, so its definitely possible to do well without attending the lecturers. Solid class overall.

Nice guy. Exams had a take-home portion, and we were required drafts for papers which was a lifesaver. Lectures complimented readings well, but he just read off slides. It's possible to get a B without attending lectures and just reading slides/papers. The exam was reasonable, try to understand the readings each week to avoid cramming.

Make sure you DO THE READING!!! So many people had a false sense of security reading one paper for their own essays and disregarding the rest. These SAME people PANICKED for the final. Otherwise, the papers/final are fairly straightforward, but like others have mentioned, TAs make or break your grade. Once again: DO THE READING.

Entertaining lecturer for PHIL120W. Lots of reading, but it isn't mandatory, only need to read specific ones for your paper (and review for final exam). Beware all papers are marked by your TA, so they matter heavily in your grade. For instance, the whole class average for one paper was 10% higher than my TA's three tutorials sections. Recommended

Very good prof and passionate about what he is teaching. Try your best to keep up with the readings or you will have to catch up all semester for the final exam. Depending on your TA will determine if you get an easy or hard marker for papers. Content-wise it was harder than the description described but mentioned many interesting topics.

Professor Guindon is a total gem. This is the 2nd philosophy class I have ever taken but he's a great guy and his lectures are always super intriguing and easy to understand. If you can, definitely recommend taking this class with him!

Difficult class - surprisingly so. If you get it, you'll be fine, but otherwise play a catchup game the entire semester. Bruno is helpful, all questions posed to him were answered in extreme detail to ensure understanding.

Bruno is a very good professor, he explains concepts clearly and really attempts to give students a chance to ask questions. He demonstrates regularly that he genuinely cares. However, it is hard to come up with questions when you do not understand what is going on. Be warned that this class has extremely difficult content, it is almost shocking.

Caution this course is very difficult. As the weeks go by it gets very hard to manage everything and keep up with both lectures and homework. Course material is not easy to grasp within one day and takes time before you understand it all. That being said, the prof is very kind and genuinely wants his students to succeed/ask for help.

Bruno is a very nice man but taking this course online was extremely difficult and I don't feel like his lectures/videos were very helpful at explaining the material. The class average was probably like 65% but it was curved thankfully. Take online with caution and be prepared to spend 12+ hours per week studying.

Took it Winter 2020 right before lockdown. His lectures were never boring and he would try to find ways to engage the class. Midterm ended up being cancelled due to Covid so grade was based off of 2 papers with drafts, 1 final paper, tutorial participation, and a take home final. As long as you do the readings, you'll do fine in the class
I am not a phil major so this was a bit hard for me BUT Bruno is a great prof. Such a nice guy and always there to help you and his lectures are not boring. 10/10 would recommend

Bruno always came to class prepared and with a willingness to explain a wide range of questions. His marking is a bit strict, but he gives excellent feedback and genuinely wants people to enjoy philosophy, and to succeed academically. Overall an excellent professor.

Absolutely zero consideration for student's mental health or stress. Does not believe in practicing any leniency whatsoever. Would not recommend any classes with him at all. Save your grade and take the course with any other professor.

Bruno always came to class prepared and was able to answer a wide variety of questions. Its nice having a Phil prof who actually seems interested in learning the subject matter. His class was heavy on readings and he marked papers within strict criteria, but he was always willing to provide pointers and provided an excellent learning environment.

The prof is well-prepared for each lecture and his class is really makes me engaged. The paper is not very hard and the final exam is fair (he has already given us paper topic and we can prepare them in advance). : )

Sometimes people stir up a ruckus in class because it's philosophy and the topics are grounds for open debate in the middle lecture, amirite? He takes the students' personalities in stride, doesn't really mind it that much when people get offensive. Able to calm people down. Don't say anything "intuitive" or obvious or else you get a look.
Bruno is a good speaker and his lecture slides are pretty easy to understand. The marking for the papers is kinda subjective and I'm not sure if it's because I got a bad TA or not.
Bruno was very passionate during lecture and was able to clarify concepts clearly using examples. Interesting course content and really makes you think.

Bruno would be a solid choice. Hilariously crazy guy though, I was talking to him about meat-eating, and he straight up said to me as an utilitarian that there are be no difference in killing/eating a human than killing/eating a pig if you were stuck on an island with no food since animals and humans are equal. I disagreed. Besides that, good prof.
